questions: Why should you stop ads for out‑of‑stock products?
What is the impact of advertising unavailable items?
qa.answer: Advertising out‑of‑stock items wastes spend, hurts Quality Score and user trust, and can degrade account performance through poor engagement signals.
implication: Automate product‑level pausing via feed rules and scripts when availability=out_of_stock.
Route budget to in‑stock, high‑margin SKUs; suppress OOS variants in Shopping feeds.
Add back‑in‑stock alerts and alternatives on PDPs to salvage demand.

questions: How do you operationalize OOS protection in Google Ads?
What tools prevent spend on unavailable SKUs?
qa.answer: Use Merchant Center feed rules, supplemental feeds, and scheduled scripts/APIs to pause SKUs, update custom labels, and adjust bids when inventory changes.
implication: Implement hourly inventory sync; alert on feed errors and disapprovals.
Leverage custom labels for availability and margin to drive bid strategies.
QA PDP schema and stock status to keep Shopping data accurate.
